title: 日本ダービーの後に・超

text_block_list.name: Caster
text_block_list.text:
Shining splendidly in first place is none other than
Narita Brian!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: Wooooo!

text_block_list.name: Caster
text_block_list.text:
The crowd is going wild! And Narita Brian, the
new Derby Champion, raises her fist in the air in
reply!

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text: Heh.

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text:
(If I shut my eyes, I can still see the that final
stretch. Everyone straining, fighting for the finish
line.)

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text:
(I feel like I'm still there, still running... More. I
want more...)

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text: I want to win!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: Wooooo!

text_block_list.name: Spectators
text_block_list.text: Brian! Brian! Brian! Brian!

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text: ...I'm back.

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text:
Thanks. But the next race is the Kikuka Sho,
where only the strongest racer can win.

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text:
The battle for the finish line continues. I can't
ease up.

text_block_list.name: Reporter
text_block_list.text:
You're just as great as I imagined, Brian! I always
knew you'd become the Derby Champion!

text_block_list.name: Reporter
text_block_list.text:
Not only that, I think you're second only to
Symboli Rudolf! I mean, five G1 wins, no pr—

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text: ...Thanks.

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text: Second to Rudolf, huh?

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text: ...Ridiculous! Who're they calling "second"?!

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text: Goals aren't to be chased after, they're to be exceeded!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
After the concert, I take Narita Brian back to the
academy, where...

text_block_list.name: Hishi Amazon
text_block_list.text: There you are.

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text: ...Amazon?

text_block_list.name: Hishi Amazon
text_block_list.text:
I won the Japanese Oaks and you won the
Japanese Derby.

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text: Huh... So you have two tiaras now?

text_block_list.name: Hishi Amazon
text_block_list.text: Yeah. And I'm getting ready to take you down.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Amazon
text_block_list.text: ...We'll settle things at the Arima Kinen.

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text: Not the Kikuka Sho?

text_block_list.name: Hishi Amazon
text_block_list.text: D-don't be ridiculous!

text_block_list.name: Hishi Amazon
text_block_list.text: I've already come this far in order to surpass you.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Amazon
text_block_list.text:
Let's finish with the absolute best one-on-one
possible.

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text: She ran off...

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text:
Should we go for the Arima Kinen after the
Classic Triple Crown, then?
choice_data_list: You'd be competing with senior-year racers.
You'd be competing with senior-year racers.

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text:
So the Kikuka Sho determines who's the strongest
in their generation, and the Arima Kinen
determines who's the strongest of all.

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text:
And still, no one can help but aim for the top.
That's what being an Umamusume is all about.

text_block_list.name: Narita Brian
text_block_list.text:
Yeah. Let's set our sights on both the Kikuka Sho
and the Arima Kinen.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
First up is the final Triple Crown tour race, the
Kikuka Sho—a 3,000-meter turf race!
